title: perfect grade evangelion
wikitext:
[]: perfect grade evangelion             Quote Post by evaunit13 » Thu Jul 09, 2009 11:59 pm does anyone have this piece? i see it all the time being sold online but i rarely see anyone actually owning it and doing a review on it. it being the first perfect grade model, rub it in the faces of gundam fans

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Fri Jul 10, 2009 2:55 am It's hard to fuck with the Gundam perfect grades. those all have a very realistic underlying metal structure that you assemble beneath the plastic plating; the metal's held together by screws, springs, and other neat things. I don't imagine the eva perfect grade could provide much more than plastic.. it wouldn't make too much sense.

[]:             Quote Post by eva0313777 » Fri Jul 10, 2009 3:13 am actually i think the eva perfect grade came out in either 1999 or 2000,but the first gundam perfect grade came out in 1998.so ha(sorry gundam fan in me is attacking the eva fan in me lol.and compared to a gundam mastergrade,the eva is quite ammature due to it's innerframe is a rubber eva body.http://www.1999.co.jp/eng/image/10019773a/20/1 the only thing that makes it "shine" is the fact it has led's for the shoulders and the umbilical cord holder,even though every pg gundam has led's and even mg's and 1/100 ng's have them

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Fri Jul 10, 2009 5:31 am A rubber frame.. well, it makes sense. I remember getting a perfect grade model of Wing Gundam Zero Custom for christmas in 2000... I was so, so stoked.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Jul 10, 2009 12:29 pm It is the first PG Evangelion Unit 01 = December 1997 RX-78-2 = November 1998 The reason few people talk about it is because it is a terrible, terrible, TERRIBLE kit. Partly because it's from 1997, this is only 2 years since the beginning of the MG line which is when the kits started getting fancy, but they're all really damn terrible by today's standards. The bigger problem is that fucking rubber sleeve. While the intentions were good, the execution was not. The task of trying to fit the inner frame into the sleeve is comparable to trying to jam a pineapple up your own ass. If you managed to succeed in that without ripping it, probably with the magic of talcum powder being the common solution, you have to get all the peg alligned and fit the damn armor on. Unless you're a masochist who plans to fully paint the thing and cement the necessary parts, stay away.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Fri Jul 10, 2009 6:39 pm Maybe they were just trying to show how much sense the armor-busting in ep. 19 makes?

Article: perfect grade evangelion
78-2 = November 1998 The reason few people talk about it is because it is a terrible, terrible, TERRIBLE kit. Partly because it's from 1997, this is only 2 years since the beginning of the MG line which is when the kits started getting fancy, but they're all really damn terrible by today's standards. The bigger problem is that fucking rubber sleeve. While the intentions were good, the execution was not. The task of trying to fit the inner frame into the sleeve is comparable to trying to jam a pineapple up your own ass. If you managed to succeed in that without ripping it, probably with the magic of talcum powder being the common solution, you have to get all the peg alligned and fit the damn armor on. Unless you're a masochist who plans to fully paint the thing and cement the necessary parts, stay away.
